headline: SIXTH CONSECUTIVE DEFERRAL — UNMISS mandate (Resolution 2779) expires April 30, 2026 (T-2 days). Despite hard-deadline protocol activation, Security Council Report What's In Blue and press.un.org return no April 25-28 entry confirming a scheduled or held renewal vote. Last comparable renewal cycle (2025) required a technical rollover (Res 2778) on April 30 before substantive Resolution 2779 passed May 8 — same pattern is plausible. Floor-limited entity (composite 0); no expressive scoring movement available regardless of vote outcome. Deferral escalated to PENDING_CHANGES.md human-review queue with explicit April 30 hard-deadline marker.
deferral_reason: UNMISS Security Council renewal vote not confirmed in publicly accessible primary sources as of 2026-04-28 scan (T-2 days from mandate expiry). What's In Blue has no current entry; press.un.org Apr 25-28 releases cover Sudan sanctions, Hormuz security, Haiti, Gaza ceasefire, Resolution 1988 amendments — not UNMISS renewal. Vote may be: (a) scheduled April 29-30 but not yet pre-announced, (b) subject to technical rollover precedent (Res 2778 in 2025), (c) experiencing protracted negotiations as in 2025 cycle. Floor-limitation methodology gap means any outcome cannot be expressed via composite movement.

evidence.source: Security Council Report What's In Blue
evidence.finding: April 16-17 routine UNMISS briefing entry only. No April 25-28 vote-pending entry as of fetch on 2026-04-28.

Entries cover Resolution 1988 amendments (4/28), Sudan sanctions Resolution 1591 additions (4/28), maritime security Hormuz (4/27), Haiti (4/23), Gaza ceasefire briefing (4/28). No UNMISS renewal entry.
Last cycle required technical rollover Resolution 2778 (April 30, 2025) before substantive Res 2779 passed May 8, 2025 — same delay pattern plausible.

Sudan Doctors Network: 1,470+ civilians + 907 military personnel + 20 doctors held in dire RSF detention conditions in el-Fasher; cholera outbreak since February; ethnic-targeted torture and execution.

evidence.source: OCHA South Sudan Humanitarian Update 1-17 April 2026
evidence.finding: 40% reduction in UNMISS protection patrols, 40 canceled human rights monitoring missions documented in window.

methodologyNote: FLOOR-LIMITATION + HARD-DEADLINE COMBINED METHODOLOGY GAP. South Sudan is at composite 0 (true floor) and the UNMISS vote outcome — whether full renewal, reduced-scope renewal, technical rollover, or mandate lapse — cannot be expressed via composite movement under current methodology. Sixth consecutive deferral. Recommended action: escalate to PENDING_CHANGES.md human-review queue; require manual override decision by April 30 to either (a) record the vote outcome as a methodology-note registered confirmation or (b) trigger emergency reassessment if mandate lapses.
